title: Tennis The Menace

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer encountering some mishap during opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation of frustration
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly integrated but routine usage

jokes.text: Homer screaming musical notes to 'Mary Had A Little Lamb'
jokes.setup: Homer apparently in pain making noises
jokes.punchline: His screams accidentally form a recognizable children's song melody
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist humor turning pain into accidental music

jokes.text: Ever notice after dialysis... you always get the munchies?
jokes.setup: Old folks talent show stand-up routine
jokes.punchline: Comparing serious medical treatment to marijuana effects
jokes.explanation: Dark medical humor with good structure but somewhat predictable

jokes.text: He's saying the stuff we all forgot.
jokes.setup: Response to elderly comedian's routine
jokes.punchline: Recognition that old age humor reflects forgotten experiences
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about aging and memory

jokes.text: And you know what I can't open? Cabinets.
jokes.setup: Building on pattern of age-related complaints
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly mundane difficulty for elderly
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective old-age observational humor

jokes.text: You know what else scares me? Everything.
jokes.setup: Pattern of specific complaints
jokes.punchline: Ultimate escalation to total fear
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in escalating to absurd extreme

jokes.text: What's new, pussycat / Ow, ow, ow, ow
jokes.setup: Elderly person performing Tom Jones song
jokes.punchline: Physical pain interrupting the lyrics in rhythm
jokes.explanation: Clever integration of physical comedy with musical performance

jokes.text: since Abe's already standing here, what the heck
jokes.setup: Announcing contest winner at talent show
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary selection based purely on convenience
jokes.explanation: Good satire of arbitrary contest judging

jokes.text: Wow! A free auto. / No, keep reading. / Oh.
jokes.setup: Abe excited about winning a 'free auto'
jokes.punchline: Reality check that it's not what he thought
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with quick misdirection and deflation

jokes.text: Hey, let's get this down to the funeral home before they try to back out.
jokes.setup: Abe won an autopsy coupon
jokes.punchline: Homer's eagerness to cash in on death benefit
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer selfishness with dark undertones

jokes.text: Sir, we prefer the term 'casket' to coffin... and 'monument' to tombstone.
jokes.setup: Funeral home sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Pompous euphemisms for death-related items
jokes.explanation: Good satire of funeral industry marketing speak

jokes.text: What funny names. [imitating airplane] He's heading into Dead Man's Curve!
jokes.setup: Looking at anti-stink spray brands
jokes.punchline: Homer making airplane noises about death products
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer childishness but somewhat forced

jokes.text: [Chuckling] Oh, the living.
jokes.setup: Homer being childish about death products
jokes.punchline: Condescending dismissal from someone who profits from death
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical and character-appropriate condescension

jokes.text: That thing got picture-in-picture?
jokes.setup: Being shown high-tech casket with 'cadaver cam'
jokes.punchline: Homer treating death like TV viewing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - treating serious situation like consumer electronics

jokes.text: Aw, geez. How come they never call traveling anymore?
jokes.setup: Watching someone presumably struggling in casket
jokes.punchline: Homer applying basketball rules to death throes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - applying sports commentary to inappropriate situation

jokes.text: optional 'weeping widow' service
jokes.setup: Listing deluxe funeral services
jokes.punchline: Professional mourning as an upsell service
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist concept but not deeply developed

jokes.text: God prefers people who travel in style
jokes.setup: Homer questioning expensive funeral costs
jokes.punchline: Religious manipulation for sales purposes
jokes.explanation: Good satire of predatory sales tactics

jokes.text: If you cheapskate me into hell, I'll haunt you good.
jokes.setup: Discussion of funeral costs
jokes.punchline: Abe threatening supernatural revenge over funeral expenses
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abe line - combining guilt, supernatural threats, and cheapness

jokes.text: Imagine this when you're sittin' on the john. [Gibberish]
jokes.setup: Abe describing how he'll haunt Homer
jokes.punchline: Specific, uncomfortable haunting scenario
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortably specific haunting threat that's perfectly in character

jokes.text: Or you could just toss him in the woods and let the wolves carry him off.
jokes.setup: Homer balking at $17,000 funeral cost
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude alternative presented casually
jokes.explanation: Shocking alternative that contrasts with previous upselling

jokes.text: We're gonna go with the wolves.
jokes.setup: Given choice between expensive funeral and wolves
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately choosing the wolves option
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer choice - completely practical and heartless

jokes.text: Dad, I love you this much. But that's just under $900.
jokes.setup: Homer expressing love for his father with gesture
jokes.punchline: Quantifying love in exact dollar amounts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant joke structure - turning emotional gesture into financial calculation

jokes.text: It'll consume as much cement as a regulation-size tennis court.
jokes.setup: Describing elaborate mausoleum features
jokes.punchline: Inadvertently giving Homer the tennis court idea
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for episode plot - accidental inspiration

jokes.text: I bet you didn't see that comin'.
jokes.setup: Homer revealing they got a tennis court instead of funeral
jokes.punchline: Homer breaking fourth wall about plot twist
jokes.explanation: Mild meta humor but not particularly sophisticated

jokes.text: try telling that to 'Killy McGee' up there
jokes.setup: Homer saying if it were up to him, Abe wouldn't die
jokes.punchline: Referring to God as 'Killy McGee'
jokes.explanation: Creative irreverent nickname for God that's very Homer

jokes.text: Then what's the one where the chicks whale on each other? / Foxy boxing? / Yes! That's what I wanted.
jokes.setup: Homer confused about tennis
jokes.punchline: He actually wanted to watch women's boxing
jokes.explanation: Good Homer confusion but somewhat predictable punchline

jokes.text: I hear Mel Brooks has one. I mean Sir Mel Brooks.
jokes.setup: Homer talking about tennis courts making houses classy
jokes.punchline: Homer pseudo-correcting himself with fake title
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about Homer's misunderstanding of titles

jokes.text: Hey, you got a tennis court? / Keep walkin', Flanders. / Will do. / Faster.
jokes.setup: Flanders asking about the tennis court
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasing hostility and Flanders' compliance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamics with good escalation

jokes.text: Sorry. I grunted way too early. [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Playing tennis with exaggerated grunting
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary about tennis grunting timing
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about tennis conventions

jokes.text: we never knew nobody with their own tennis rink.
jokes.setup: Commenting on having fun at tennis
jokes.punchline: Calling tennis court a 'rink' showing ignorance
jokes.explanation: Simple class-based humor about sports terminology

jokes.text: So what happens now? Is the food free, or do we pay someone?
jokes.setup: After playing tennis at someone's house
jokes.punchline: Not understanding basic social conventions
jokes.explanation: Good class commentary on social expectations

jokes.text: That's how you learn- By asking, you dumb-ass.
jokes.setup: One character asking about party etiquette
jokes.punchline: Other defending asking while insulting him
jokes.explanation: Good working-class banter with mild contradiction

jokes.text: But the real winners here are Marge's hors d'oeuvres.
jokes.setup: Kent commenting on tennis match results
jokes.punchline: TV anchor making cheesy transition to food compliment
jokes.explanation: Good Kent Brockman characterization - professional broadcaster making awkward social small talk.

jokes.text: 'I guess you could say it's my racket.' / I guess you could say I'm Iraqi.
jokes.setup: Kent delivering tennis pun about his witty remarks
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misunderstanding and making random statement
jokes.explanation: Good Homer non-sequitur that completely misses the pun structure

jokes.text: Are you here to make a bust of some kind?
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum at the door
jokes.punchline: Marge using 'bust' in sculpture sense not arrest sense
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay that fits Marge's innocent character

jokes.text: checking to make sure you're having a crime-free morning
jokes.setup: Police visit to the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous proactive policing concept
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum absurdity about police procedures

jokes.text: What's that, Lou? Lettin' that tennis court go to waste.
jokes.setup: Wiggum asking what would be a real crime
jokes.punchline: Lou's transparent hint about wanting to play tennis
jokes.explanation: Good setup and transparent motive revelation

jokes.text: he had it converted into a human chessboard
jokes.setup: Explaining why Mr. Burns doesn't have his tennis court anymore
jokes.punchline: Bizarre rich person conversion project
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns-style eccentric rich person absurdity

jokes.text: Hey, Burns has been gone for a while. Let's make a run for it.
jokes.setup: Playing human chess for Mr. Burns
jokes.punchline: Smithers suggesting escape from their chess piece duties
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to human chessboard concept

jokes.text: Hey, check it out. I'm surfing the net.
jokes.setup: Moe doing something physical with the tennis net
jokes.punchline: Pun on internet 'surfing the net' while literally on tennis net
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but fairly obvious wordplay

jokes.text: John Rocker, O.J. Simpson, Dorf
jokes.setup: Homer listing sports' lovable clowns
jokes.punchline: Grouping controversial figures with harmless comedy character
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor mixing serious and silly 'clowns'

jokes.text: Yeah, but this time we got the right address.
jokes.setup: Chief saying they busted crack house last night
jokes.punchline: Revealing previous raid was at wrong location
jokes.explanation: Good police incompetence humor with dark implications

jokes.text: You mean the L.A. Clippers of backyard tennis?
jokes.setup: Lou hearing Wiggum plays tennis with the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Comparing Simpsons to famously bad basketball team
jokes.explanation: Excellent sports metaphor with perfect economy

jokes.text: They're easier to beat than a suspect in shackles.
jokes.setup: Describing how easy the Simpsons are to beat at tennis
jokes.punchline: Comparing to police brutality scenario
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about police practices fitting the characters

jokes.text: That's a joke, Lou- It's on the Simpsons and their easy 'beatability.'
jokes.setup: Lou making police brutality reference
jokes.punchline: Wiggum awkwardly clarifying the joke's intent
jokes.explanation: Good character moment of Wiggum missing the dark humor

jokes.text: No! Aw, this home testing kit saved me a fortune.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if he has cooties
jokes.punchline: Homer using medical test kit for childhood playground concern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - applying adult solution to childish worry

jokes.text: Well, that's bad news for Dingbat Charlie. He's gonna be crushed.
jokes.setup: Marge saying they're the laughingstock of the town
jokes.punchline: Homer deflecting to imaginary person who apparently held that title
jokes.explanation: Great Homer deflection with invented character name

jokes.text: Oh. Well, that's bad news for Dingbat Charlie. He's gonna be crushed.
jokes.setup: Learning they're the laughingstock of town
jokes.punchline: Worrying about impact on imaginary rival laughingstock
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - creating imaginary competition even for being mocked. High originality and perfect character voice.

jokes.text: I'll practice till my elbow is swollen and discolored.
jokes.setup: Homer promising to take tennis seriously
jokes.punchline: Overly graphic description of dedication
jokes.explanation: Good Homer escalation - taking simple request to uncomfortable extreme. Solid character writing.

jokes.text: Time of death, 10:15. [Laughs]
jokes.setup: Operating outside with tennis ball explosion nearby
jokes.punchline: Doctor casually pronouncing death time and laughing
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Hibbert inappropriate cheerfulness about death

jokes.text: Now, that's the kind of nagging I can live with.
jokes.setup: Lisa telling Homer to have another beer
jokes.punchline: Homer appreciating 'nagging' that enables his drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic about preferring enabling to actual nagging

jokes.text: everyone's gonna think you're playing in the tournament. / I am playing in the tournament. / It's gonna be tough without a partner.
jokes.setup: Homer seeing Bart in tennis outfit
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious assumption Bart is playing alone
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer's confusion about partnerships

jokes.text: Dude, I think the lady's made her decision.
jokes.setup: Homer protesting that Marge is his partner
jokes.punchline: Bart treating family dynamics like a casual dating situation
jokes.explanation: Good Bart sass treating serious family moment casually

jokes.text: Don't touch me. Your hands feel like salad tongs.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to console hurt Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's oddly specific and harsh rejection metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer insult - specific, unexpected, and cruelly accurate

jokes.text: We can wipe out B.C. in our lifetime. / I don't care about B.C. I care about M.E. - My enjoyment.
jokes.setup: Bart talking about wiping out balcony collapse
jokes.punchline: Homer's selfish wordplay turning BC into ME
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer wordplay that perfectly captures his selfishness

jokes.text: Well, maybe five bucks would refresh my memory.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if Homer remembers the story of Oedipus
jokes.punchline: Homer treating basic knowledge as premium information
jokes.explanation: Good Homer greed trying to monetize education

jokes.text: Ugh! Who pays for that wedding?
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining Oedipus married his mother
jokes.punchline: Homer focusing on practical wedding expense concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - missing profound meaning for mundane concern

jokes.text: This session's over. / This session's under. / Good-bye. / Bad-bye.
jokes.setup: Lisa ending their therapy-like conversation
jokes.punchline: Homer's petty word reversals in response
jokes.explanation: Childish Homer wordplay showing his immaturity

jokes.text: Darling, you're so much 'handsomer' than my first husband.
jokes.setup: Homer's paranoid dream about Marge leaving him for Bart
jokes.punchline: Dream Marge using incorrect comparative form
jokes.explanation: Mild grammar humor within dream sequence. Functional but not particularly strong.

jokes.text: Now serve. / Ew! Dad, I don't want to enter the tournament just to hurt Mom.
jokes.setup: Homer training Lisa to aim for Marge's heart
jokes.punchline: Lisa's moral objection to Homer's revenge scheme
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Homer's pettiness and Lisa's ethics

jokes.text: Your backhand looks like a rusty gate.
jokes.setup: Lisa defending herself as better than Bart at tennis
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly harsh and specific insult from sweet Lisa
jokes.explanation: Great unexpected nastiness from usually sweet Lisa

jokes.text: Dear Lisa. Psych! Psych, psych, psych. Signed, super psych.
jokes.setup: Bart delivering telegram to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fake telegram just to say 'psych'
jokes.explanation: Good Bart prank with appropriately silly execution

jokes.text: This? Oh, it's just fluid collecting. [Sloshing]
jokes.setup: Someone asking about novelty hand
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's actual medical condition with sound effect
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor with uncomfortable reveal

jokes.text: I hope I don't get arrested for... racket earring. / Aw, what do you want? I'm not gonna do 'A' material for charity.
jokes.setup: Krusty making tennis pun at charity event
jokes.punchline: Krusty defending his bad joke by explaining it's deliberately low-effort
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about performer effort at charity events

jokes.text: Listen, I need a new doubles partner. My little girl's a lead weight.
jokes.setup: Homer approaching Venus Williams for partnership
jokes.punchline: Homer casually insulting Lisa to recruit professional
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer callousness toward his own daughter

jokes.text: You're dumping your own daughter? / Yeah, but only to crush my wife and son.
jokes.setup: Venus Williams shocked at Homer abandoning Lisa
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual explanation making it worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic where his explanation makes things worse

jokes.text: Sorry. That's Homer and Venus Simpson.
jokes.setup: Announcer correcting team roster
jokes.punchline: Venus Williams now identified as a Simpson
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment treating pro athlete as family member

jokes.text: Let's just say I'm replacing you.
jokes.setup: Lisa saying 'dumping' is a harsh word
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt correction that's not actually gentler
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer 'diplomacy' that's actually more brutal

jokes.text: [Groaning] I just ate a personal pizza.
jokes.setup: Serena being assigned to play after partner switch
jokes.punchline: Professional athlete's mundane excuse for poor performance
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about athlete making excuses

jokes.text: I'm 'un-dumpable.' / [Man] Yoink. / My tennis stick.
jokes.setup: Bart proud of not being dumped by anyone
jokes.punchline: Immediately being abandoned and not even knowing tennis terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing and Bart calling racket a 'stick'

jokes.text: Which one am I, Serena or Venus? / You're Andre. I'm Serena. / You're a girl.
jokes.setup: Homer confused about which Williams sister he is
jokes.punchline: Homer calling Andre Agassi a girl
jokes.explanation: Good Homer confusion leading to accidental insult

jokes.text: It's better to watch stuff than to do stuff.
jokes.setup: Family watching professional tennis players
jokes.punchline: Homer's lazy life philosophy about spectatorship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer wisdom that's both lazy and arguably true

tmdb_overview: While making funeral arrangements for Grampa's future, the funeral planner pitches the idea of a mausoleum that uses the same amount of cement as a full-sized tennis court. This prompts Homer to instead build a tennis court in his backyard, initially mistaking the sport for "Foxy Boxing". The tennis court makes Marge and Homer popular in town, but it turns out that this is only because Homer's inferior tennis ability makes them an easy target. Bart on the other hand, shines as a natural.
